mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in industrial production (INDPRO) and decline in manufacturing inventories (RSAFS) signals strong supply-side momentum that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative when INDPRO rises >2% and RSAFS falls >1% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates economic weakness offset by real estate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when GDPC1 declines and HOUST rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payrolls (PAYEMS) below 50k indicates weak labor market performance that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS declines below 50k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining consumer confidence (CPIAUCSL) indicates divergent demand signals that produce negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive when HOUST rises and CPIAUCSL falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 indicates weakening supply chains that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when INDPRO falls below 100 for three consecutive months.
